# Story 6.0b AC3 — release-gate allow-list of known dependency vulnerability warnings.
#
# Each non-blank, non-comment line is an exact-line allow-list entry that the
# CI vuln gate compares (via `comm -23`) against the de-duplicated
# `dotnet build` warning lines (NU1902 + NU1903), already truncated to the
# canonical `NUxxxx: Package '<name>' <version>` substring before comparison.
# Any captured warning whose canonical substring does NOT byte-match a line
# below fails the build with `::error::Unexpected vulnerability warning…`.
# (A patch bump that changes the version, e.g. MailKit 4.15.1 → 4.15.2,
# trips the gate by design — re-review the upstream advisory and decide
# whether the new version still has the open CVE before updating this file.)
#
# When this gate fires, ONE of two things is happening:
#   (a) Your story bumped a dependency (or an Umbraco patch landed) that
#       introduced a new transitive vuln. Investigate at the upstream issue
#       tracker, decide on resolve-via-bump vs document-with-target-date, then
#       update this file in the same diff.
#   (b) An Umbraco patch silently fixed a previously-catalogued NU190x and
#       the warning no longer fires. Remove the now-stale entry from this
#       file AND update `docs/dependency-status.md` § Vulnerability warnings
#       to mark the row resolved.
#
# Both cases require an explicit edit to this file alongside the underlying
# change. Do NOT add a wildcard / regex pattern that would silence "all NU190x"
# warnings — that defeats the gate.
#
# See `docs/dependency-status.md` for the canonical narrative + target review
# dates for each entry below.

NU1902: Package 'MailKit' 4.15.1
NU1903: Package 'System.Security.Cryptography.Xml' 8.0.0
